blocks.body: A three-year commerce degree covering accounting, finance, taxation, economics and business law. It's the base for professional courses like CA, CMA and CS, and for analyst and management careers — often followed by an M.Com or an MBA.

blocks.title: How you get in
blocks.body: Mostly 10+2 (commerce preferred) on merit; some universities use an entrance test (e.g. DU CUET).

blocks.title: At a glance
items: The default launchpad for CA, CMA, CS and CFA aspirants.
Strong fit for accounting, finance, banking and analyst roles.
An MBA or M.Com is a common next step into management.
